stories.headline: Deadly air raids across Ukraine after warnings of Russian strike wave
stories.summary: Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv hit, with apartment buildings among the targets according to officials, after president said intelligence predicted ‘massive strike’ Russian air raids on major Ukrainian centres including Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv killed at least nine people and wounded dozens by early morning on Tuesday, authorities said.In Kyiv, at least four people killed and 29 others were injured across the capital, said Tymur Tkachenko, head of the c

stories.headline: Massive Russian attack on cities across Ukraine kills at least ten people
stories.summary: Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko says two high-rise apartment buildings have been hit and there are fears people are trapped under rubble.

stories.headline: Rafael Grossi: the next Iran nuclear deal will look very different
stories.summary: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi says the 2015 Iran nuclear deal is no longer a workable model.

stories.headline: Massive overnight Russian missiles rock Ukraine, killing at least 11 people, authorities say
stories.summary: The latest attacks come days after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Russia was preparing a "massive new strike" on the country.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump talks to Hezbollah, Israel as Lebanon fighting surges
stories.summary: Israeli National Security Minister Ben-Gvir says 'time' to say 'no' to Trump and continue striking Hezbollah in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Australia politics live: Chalmers says Wilson ‘not the sharpest tool in the shed’ in question time; government spent $3.8m on personal protection for CFMEU administrator
stories.summary: Follow today’s news liveGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe foreign minister, Penny Wong, has announced new sanctions by the commonwealth over three Israeli individuals and four entities in response to “escalating settler violence against Palestinians in the West Bank”.The entities will receive targeted financial sanctions while the individuals will receive travel bans to Australia.For the first time, designated entitie

stories.headline: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: Trump Backs Off Plan for $1.8 Billion Fund That Drew Political Backlash
stories.summary: The fund has drawn backlash from critics who said it was a scheme to reward President Trump’s political allies with public benefits.

stories.headline: Mexican protesters clash with police ahead of the 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Teachers marched in Mexico demanding better pay and pensions, warning of further protests ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

stories.headline: Google parent Alphabet to sell $80bn in stock to fund AI plans
stories.summary: US tech giant says fundraising drive includes deal to sell $10bn of stock to Berkshire Hathaway.

stories.headline: The world will be 100 million cancer workers short by 2050, according to Lancet
stories.summary: A new report warns the world is heading towards a cancer care crisis, with cases sharply rising and the workforce failing to keep pace.

stories.headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
stories.summary: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

stories.headline: EV sales set records in 37 countries as consumers flee high gas prices
The 2026 World Cup: Billions promised but will the economic boom arrive?
stories.summary: With the kick-off less than ten days away, the largest World Cup in history is set to inject tens of billions into the hosting economies. Yet, analysts are estimating that the relative impact will be marginal.

stories.headline: Australian ex-minister launches crowd-funded inquiry into Aukus submarine deal
stories.summary: Inquiry will look at whether Australia will receive the submarines and whether it will make the country safer.

stories.headline: China cracks down on 'ghost kitchens' in food delivery apps
stories.summary: "Ghost kitchens", which are listed on apps but have no physical stores, have raised food safety fears.

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank’s comeback
stories.summary: After decades of upheaval, the lender that wanted to take on Wall Street has scaled back its ambitions to focus on Europe

stories.headline: How did Spain’s unemployment rate converge with Finland’s?
stories.summary: Migration policy and labour market reforms are affecting joblessness — but in very different ways

stories.headline: Tencent moves closer to launching AI agent for China’s most-used app
stories.summary: Maker of WeChat has fallen behind domestic rivals in artificial intelligence models

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Drawn Into Probe Over €5.7 Billion Bolloré Sale
stories.summary: Morgan Stanley risks being embroiled in a French criminal case by a group of communities in Africa that suspect the US bank helped Vincent Bolloré launder money stemming from the disposal of an allegedly corrupt €5.7 billion ($6.6 billion) asset.

stories.headline: State Street Head Trader Says Won May Rebound in Second Half
stories.summary: The South Korean won could rebound in the second half of the year as foreign selling of local equities eases and persistent current-account surpluses support the currency, according to a State Street Bank and Trust Co. trader with more than 25 years at the firm.

stories.headline: Japan’s 10-Year Bond Yield Drops After Auction Sees Firm Demand
stories.summary: Japan’s 10-year government bond yield dropped after an auction of the tenor drew firm demand as investors were attracted to high yields despite the ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Asian Markets Weigh Iran Risk, AI Rally | The Asia Trade 6/2/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Sydney with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China’s Solar Industry Launches Space Alliance With Few Details
stories.summary: Chinese solar manufacturers and research institutes launched an alliance on Tuesday to promote solar energy in space, a potential new avenue for growth in an industry beset by terrestrial overcapacity.

stories.headline: Arm CEO Haas on Agentic AI and Taiwan's Ecosystem
stories.summary: Arm CEO Rene Haas explains how Taiwan's partner ecosystem is critical for AI development. He also discussed how the rapid rise of agentic AI will trigger immense growth in CPU demand. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Job Cuts or Job Change? AI Debate Heats Up
stories.summary:
A Mercer survey finds most Australian firms expect AI to cut up to 20% of jobs within two years, as worker confidence falls and fears rise. Speaking on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade, Mercer Workforce Solutions Leader Cynthia Cottrell breaks down the reality: rising restructuring, uneven AI adoption, and a workforce still playing catch-up.
What it means for jobs, graduates, and the future of work. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: What's Next After Short Seller Andrew Left Found Guilty
stories.summary: Andrew Left, one of the world’s most prominent short sellers, was found guilty of securities fraud by a US federal jury. Bloomberg's Erik Larson reports on the latest. (Corrects name in headline.) (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: PBOC Cuts Cash Operation to Record Low as Bond Rally Deepens
stories.summary: China’s central bank reduced the size of its daily open-market operation to a record low, extending efforts to absorb excess cash as a rally in bonds drove benchmark yields to their lowest since August.

stories.headline: China’s LNG Imports Rebound in May as Buyers Prepare for Summer
stories.summary: China’s liquefied natural gas imports rebounded in May as the world’s largest buyer stepped up purchases ahead of peak summer demand, reversing a months-long decline following disruptions to Middle East supplies.

stories.headline: Minnesota Republicans Hold Moment of Silence for Derek Chauvin
stories.summary: Delegates to the Minnesota Republican Party’s convention voted to hold a moment of silence for Derek Chauvin, who was convicted of murdering George Floyd.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.headline: AI giant Anthropic says it plans to list on US stock market
stories.summary: The AI company behind Claude is set to offer the public shares of stock sometime this year.

stories.headline: Denmark gets new government as Greenland crisis persists
stories.summary: End of election deadlock will see PM Frederiksen continue her stewardship of response to Trump's Greenland lust.

stories.headline: Trump halts $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponisation’ fund amid bipartisan backlash
stories.summary: The announcement comes after Trump met with congressional Republicans over concerns about his settlement with the IRS.

stories.headline: UN urges calm as Israel advances into Lebanon and Iran threatens counter-fronts
stories.summary: The UN has called on all sides to respect the ceasefire following an Israeli military push into Lebanon, which threatens to derail US-Iran peace talks. Israel ordered strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs as Hezbollah targeted Haifa, prompting Iran to threaten the opening of new fronts.

stories.headline: Trump administration will abide by ruling halting $1.8bn 'anti-weaponisation' fund
stories.summary: The justice department says it  "disagrees strongly" with a court decision to halt the establishment of the fund.

stories.headline: Nigeria police warn against reprisal attacks against South Africans
stories.summary: Tensions have been rising in South Africa in recent weeks following several anti-migrant protests.

stories.headline: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity

stories.headline: Protesters call on Kenyan government to halt femicide crisis
stories.summary: Advocacy groups have given the government 40 days to intervene to deal with the plague of gender-based violence.

stories.headline: AI giant Anthropic files for US IPO as investors bet big on AI future
stories.summary: Anthropic, which operates AI chatbot Claude, did not disclose the size or the terms of the offering.

stories.headline: Hungarian PM threatens to oust Orbán-era president
stories.summary: Hungary's president has refused Prime Minister Péter Magyar's demands to step aside, setting up a constitutional clash.

stories.headline: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
stories.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

stories.headline: Poland's controversial 'Highway to Hel' 666 bus revived
stories.summary: Poland's seaside resort called Hel is getting its 666 bus service back, despite earlier opposition from Christian groups.

stories.headline: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival
stories.summary: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.

stories.headline: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.headline: Stocks Erase Losses to Hold Near Record, Oil Slips: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks erased their losses as investors returned to AI-linked shares after a brief pullback in one of the market’s strongest trades this year.

stories.headline: Elliott Buys Stake in Top Australia Gold Miner Northern Star
stories.summary: Elliott Investment Management LP has built a stake in Australia’s biggest gold miner Northern Star Resources Ltd. and is pushing for a strategic review, including a potential sale.

stories.headline: Andrew Left Found Guilty in Case That Spooked Short Sellers
stories.summary: Famed short seller Andrew Left faces the possibility of decades behind bars after being found guilty of using disingenuous social media posts to manipulate stocks, in a landmark case that threatens to chill a broader trading strategy loathed by corporate executives.

stories.headline: Previewing California’s Primary Elections and High-Stakes Governor’s Race
stories.summary: The deep-blue state’s primary elections will show how angry voters are at the status quo.

stories.headline: Demonstrators Cannot Be Forced to Take Down ‘86-47’ Flag, Judge Rules
stories.summary: A federal judge wrote that the phrase, which also led to charges against the former F.B.I. director, James Comey, did not appear to constitute a true threat.

stories.headline: Cyclist praised for rescuing four people who nearly drowned in Ruhr, Germany
stories.summary: Family were enjoying a waterside barbecue in Bochum before members ended up in jeopardy in riverA cyclist has been praised for having “stepped in decisively” and rescuing four members of a family who nearly drowned in the Ruhr River in Germany during a waterside barbecue that almost ended in tragedy.The family of eight had set themselves up on the riverbank in the western city of Bochum, the local fire brigade reported, but the gathering took a p

stories.headline: Violent protests erupt in Chile over government cuts of social programmes
stories.summary: Labour unions and student groups protested in Chile as President Kast delivered his first State of the Nation.

stories.headline: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

stories.headline: Secret tunnels and unregistered workers: China's coal mine disaster is a reminder of darker days
stories.summary: China's worst coal mining disaster in 15 years comes amid an ambitious pivot towards green energy.

stories.headline: Caribbean hot sauce producers warn of shortages and higher prices
stories.summary: Manufacturers in Jamaica say the key chilli peppers they need are in limited supply.

stories.headline: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri provinceContaining the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against

stories.headline: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance.Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off

stories.headline: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
stories.summary: After a day of uncertainty, the government confirms the team will depart for Mexico on Monday.

stories.headline: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.

stories.summary: Minister at Singapore defence summit also reveals Australia to buy only secondhand Aukus submarines from USFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe defence minister, Richard Marles, has said the “seabed is a battlefield” in a combative speech urging Beijing to be more transparent about its maritime operations, and taking aim at weak international controls over so-called “

stories.headline: US not 'turning back' on Asia allies, but expects them to boost defence, says Hegseth
stories.summary: The US defense secretary is questioned about US commitment, including further arms deals, at an Asian security summit in Singapore.

stories.headline: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
stories.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
stories.summary: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.

stories.headline: Mumbai's famed dabbawalas fed millions for over 100 years - now they are disappearing
stories.summary: Dabbawalas, who deliver home-cooked meals, are leaving the trade as remote work and rising costs threaten their future.

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
stories.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: Learning from Ukraine war, Hezbollah is now using fibre-optic drones to hit Israel
stories.summary: Fibre-optic drones are now Hezbollah's primary weapon against Israel's soldiers and civilians.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

stories.headline: Bolivian Congress allows deployment of troops to quell protests
stories.summary: Lawmakers who back the measure say it is key to restoring normality after four weeks of road blocks.
